mnemonics.title: Decision Parameter Update
mnemonics.content: When P is Negative, Move Only Horizontal (N) - Pk+1 = Pk + 2Δy. When P is Positive, Move Diagonal (D) - Pk+1 = Pk + 2Δy - 2Δx. Remember: N = North (only up), D = Diagonal (up-right)

acronyms.usage: The fundamental scan conversion algorithm for drawing lines
Another line drawing algorithm that uses floating-point arithmetic, slower than BLA

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a balance scale: Pk represents which side (above or below the line) the midpoint falls. If below (negative), pick lower pixel; if above (positive), pick upper pixel.
Think of the line as a narrow path and pixels as stepping stones. At each step, choose the stone that keeps you closest to the path.
P = 2Δy - Δx: Think of it as '2 times vertical change minus horizontal change' - the imbalance between moving vertical vs horizontal.

analogies.analogy: Drawing a line on graph paper by coloring squares - you must choose which squares to color to best approximate the straight line.
Like a thermostat deciding whether to turn heating on or off based on whether the temperature is above or below the setpoint.
Using a measuring tape with only whole numbers (integer) vs fractional markings (floating point) - whole numbers are faster to read but less precise.
